title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to remote sensing
Introduction to remote sensing: Definition, History, Principle, Advantage,
Challenges,
Scope;
Satellite
Remote
Sensing:
Principle,
Sensor
measurement, resolutions, Basic Remote Sensing unit using Landsat data:
History, Different types, Landsat Accessing level, Selection, Landsat
calibration

title: Module 2
content:
Introduction to Image Processing
Image: relevance, properties, Image Processing techniques: filtering
pixels, applying algorithms, colour palettes, Practical software: SNAP,
downloading, importing, creating the image, manipulating, filter, NDVI
Algorithm.

title: Module 3
content:
Introduction to QGIS and Water cycle
GIS, installation, and packages, QGIS: Importing remote sensing data into
QGIS, Data handling techniques, CRS Adjustments within QGIS, Saving
projects.
Microwave data for water cycle monitoring, applications, coastal water,
Bathymetric data,microwave signatures, water quality and protection

title: Module 4
content:
Urban Environments and Landscape evolution
Introduction to optical signatures of urban surfaces, Thermal signatures of
urban surfaces, Applications, Creep, dynamics, Air quality, Time series
analysis for landscape evolution monitoring, Techniques, microwave data,
applications.
Future of Remote Sensing: Instruments, Data, Satellite, Cloud processing,
analysis, software, applications.
